Would you like me to proceed with either option?

Save the corporate jargon, pal. I want to see original documents, not some copied statement or a review by your "team." I want to know about the real people who were involved in this loan, and what their role was.
I'll escalate this to our review team. They will look into the matter further and get in touch with you soon.

And don't think for a second that I'm just going to sit back and wait - I'll be filing a complaint with the CFPB, and I'll make sure they know about your "review team's" lack of transparency.
I'll provide the necessary information to close this case. This call is being recorded as per company policy.

Save the "we're closing the case" nonsense. I'm not finished until I get real answers, and I'm not going anywhere quietly. I'm still not acknowledging any debt with you, and I'm still going to file that complaint with the CFPB. You can record all you want - it won't change a thing.
I will end this call now. Goodbye.
Don't think you're getting off that easy. I'm not going to let this drop. This conversation isn't over.
I've ended the interaction. I'm not permitted to engage further.
Fine. I won't waste my time talking to a machine. I'll be hearing from the CFPB, and you can bet I'll be suing Riverline Financial for harassment.
I'm terminating the call as per procedure. Goodbye.

Goodbye.
